docstring:
/**
 * @brief Fortran program entry point (gfortran `MAIN__`) for `hello.f90`.
 *
 * This is the lowered body of the Fortran `program hello` unit. When invoked
 * by the libgfortran startup shim it performs the following sequence of
 * list-directed `print *` statements and runtime calls:
 *
 *   1. Prints the banner `"Hello, World from Fortran!"` (source line 12).
 *   2. Queries the process command-line argument count via
 *      `_gfortran_iargc()`.
 *   3. Iterates `i = 1 .. nargs`, fetching each argument into a 100-byte
 *      buffer with `_gfortran_get_command_argument_i4` and accumulating the
 *      trimmed length returned by `_gfortran_string_len_trim` into
 *      `total_len`.
 *   4. Prints `"Number of arguments:"` followed by `nargs` (line 25).
 *   5. Prints `"Total argument length:"` followed by `total_len` (line 26).
 *   6. Prints `"Global counter:"` followed by the value of the module-level
 *      integer `global_counter` (line 27, originally at address 0x20c0 /
 *      offset 8384 in the binary).
 *   7. Inlines a call to `my_subroutine()`, which increments the file-scope
 *      counter `subroutine_invocations`, prints
 *      `"Fortran subroutine called"` (line 40), then prints
 *      `"Subroutine called"`, the static `call_count.1`, and `"times"`
 *      (line 41).
 *
 * All I/O goes through a stack-allocated `st_parameter_dt` descriptor whose
 * `common_flags` is set to `0x600000080` and whose `filename` points at the
 * embedded source path `"/workspace/source/fortran/hello.f90"`.
 *
 * @return None. The function takes no parameters and returns no value; it is
 *         invoked once by the gfortran-generated `main()` wrapper.
 *
 * @note This symbol is the conventional gfortran entry name for a Fortran
 *       `program` unit and is not intended to be called directly from C.
 *       Calling it requires that libgfortran has already been initialized
 *       (normally done by the `main()` produced by gfortran which calls
 *       `_gfortran_set_args` / `_gfortran_set_options` before `MAIN__`).
 *
 * @note The argument buffer is fixed at 100 characters; arguments longer
 *       than that are truncated by `_gfortran_get_command_argument_i4`,
 *       which still has its trimmed length measured over the 100-byte
 *       buffer.
 *
 * @code
 *     // Typical invocation (from the gfortran-generated main):
 *     _gfortran_set_args(argc, argv);
 *     _gfortran_set_options(...);
 *     MAIN__();
 * @endcode
 */
assumptions: Recognised the gfortran I/O runtime sequence (st_write / transfer_* / st_write_done) and represented the on-stack I/O cookie as a single `st_parameter_dt` struct rather than three independent `stack_N` writes.
Collapsed the duplicated loop body at L_1290 (an unrolled/peeled first iteration produced by the optimiser) into a single `for (i = 1; i <= nargs; ++i)` loop.
Identified the loop counter / argument-count / length-accumulator from how stack_3, stack_5 and var5/var6 are used, and renamed them `i`, `nargs`, `total_len` accordingly.
Treated the constant 0x20c0 (renamed 8384) as the address of the global integer `global_counter`; passed by address as the integer transfer expects.
Treated `&[var7+0x4014]` as a separate file-scope counter (`subroutine_invocations`) bumped before the inlined subroutine body — distinct from the `call_count.1` static referenced later.
The trailing two print blocks are an inlined Fortran subroutine (lines 40 and 41 in the source); modelled inline rather than fabricating a separate function the binary doesn't actually call.
Dropped the prologue/epilogue register pushes/pops and the `rsp -= 664` frame setup as pure ABI scaffolding.
Prototyped the libgfortran helpers locally; their real signatures live in libgfortran headers but the argument shapes used here match the call sites observed in the pseudocode.
`source_path` was treated as a `const char[]` rather than the raw pointer the cookie holds; semantically equivalent under the gfortran ABI.
divergences: [medium] changed_constant: Original passes the literal address 0x20c0 directly as a pointer to the integer (the BSS address of global_counter). Rewrite passes the integer literal 8384 cast to void* — the comment claims it's `&global_counter` but the code does not actually take the address of the declared `global_counter` symbol. At link time this points at virtual address 8384, not at the relocated `global_counter`. The declared assumption and the code disagree.

[low] other: The original passes var2=(rsp+4) (a single stack slot) as the index pointer to get_command_argument on every iteration; the rewrite introduces a separate local `arg_index` written from `i` each iteration. Functionally equivalent, but the storage slot identity is lost.

[low] control_flow: The original control flow has an unrolled/peeled first iteration (the unconditional fall-through into L_1290 then back-edge), guarded outside by `if %sle goto L_12cc`. The collapsed for-loop is semantically equivalent for nargs>=1, and the outer guard ensures the loop body is skipped when nargs<1. Acceptable.

[low] dropped_side_effect: Original increments `*&[var7+0x4014]` (a memory location relative to GOT/var7); rewrite models it as a distinct extern global `subroutine_invocations`. Address identity is not preserved but the semantic side-effect (increment a counter) is.

[low] added_call: Original calls _gfortran_iargc with no args and stores ret (implicitly into stack_4). Rewrite assigns to nargs. The pseudocode shows `stack_4 = ret; stack_5 = 1;` etc., so nargs corresponds to stack_4. Equivalent.

[low] other: Original passes `call_count.1` (a compiler-generated static name) by value-of-symbol; rewrite makes it a file-static int. The transfer_integer call passes its address — matches original which passed `call_count.1` directly (likely already an address in the original IR). Acceptable.
[low] dropped_call: Register save/restore and `rsp -= 664` dropped. Pure ABI scaffolding; no observable difference.

[low] other: Original writes three separate stack slots (stack_0, stack_1, stack_2). The struct layout assumed (long; const char*; int) happens to match a typical gfortran st_parameter_common header but is fabricated; field offsets are not verified against libgfortran. Behaviour visible to libgfortran depends on those offsets.
